text:
WHOLE BODY FDG PET-CT WITH CONTRAST-ENHANCED CT OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS
CONCURRENT MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM

INDICATION: 55-year-old female with newly diagnosed malignant melanoma, primary site dorsal trunk, with known right axillary lymph node involvement on pathologic evaluation. Referred for initial staging workup. Evaluate for extent of nodal and distant metastatic disease.

TECHNIQUE:
PET-CT: Whole-body FDG PET-CT performed from vertex of skull to mid-thigh following intravenous administration of 12.8 mCi F-18 fluorodeoxyglucose. Blood glucose at time of injection: 91 mg/dL. Uptake time approximately 60 minutes. Attenuation-corrected and non-attenuation-corrected images reconstructed and reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. CT component performed with intravenous iodinated contrast (Omnipaque 350, 120 mL, power injector). Non-contrast and contrast-enhanced CT acquired in standard portal venous phase.

MRI Brain: Standard multiplanar sequences performed at 3T including pre- and post-gadolinium T1-weighted (MPRAGE), T2-FLAIR, diffusion-weighted imaging, and susceptibility-weighted imaging. Gadolinium contrast (Gadavist 15 mL IV) administered without adverse event.

COMPARISON: No prior cross-sectional imaging available for comparison.

FINDINGS:

PRIMARY SITE / DORSAL TRUNK:
Post-procedural changes are noted at the mid-dorsal skin/subcutaneous tissue at the site of prior excisional biopsy of the primary lesion. There is a small area of superficial soft tissue disruption with mild adjacent FDG uptake, SUVmax approximately 3.8, consistent with post-biopsy inflammatory change. No discrete residual hypermetabolic soft tissue mass is identified at the primary biopsy site. A small adjacent area of skin thickening is noted.

HEAD AND NECK LYMPH NODES:
No cervical, supraclavicular, or submandibular lymphadenopathy identified. No FDG-avid nodes in the head and neck at this time.

AXILLARY LYMPH NODES:
Right axilla: A cluster of enlarged, FDG-avid lymph nodes is present. The dominant node measures 4.2 cm in short axis with heterogeneous internal architecture and central hypodensity on CT consistent with necrosis. SUVmax 18.4. Several adjacent smaller satellite nodes are also FDG-avid, the largest measuring approximately 1.8 cm. The dominant nodal mass demonstrates ill-defined margins with stranding into the adjacent fibrofatty tissue, suggesting extranodal extension.

Left axilla: Multiple FDG-avid lymph nodes. Dominant left axillary node measures 1.8 cm in short axis, SUVmax 9.2. Additional smaller left axillary nodes without definite enlargement show mildly increased FDG activity (SUVmax 3.2-4.1), indeterminate but suspicious.

INTRATHORACIC LYMPH NODES:
No hilar, mediastinal, or internal mammary lymphadenopathy identified. No FDG-avid intrathoracic nodes.

INGUINAL / ILIAC LYMPH NODES:
Right inguinal: Enlarged FDG-avid node measuring 2.1 cm in short axis, SUVmax 7.6. Additional smaller right inguinal nodes show mild metabolic activity.
Left inguinal: FDG-avid node measuring 1.4 cm in short axis, SUVmax 5.3.
Bilateral external iliac and obturator nodal chains: No pathologically enlarged or FDG-avid nodes.

LUNGS AND PLEURA:
The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidation. No pleural effusions. No FDG-avid pulmonary parenchymal lesions. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level. No hilar or mediastinal lymphadenopathy as noted above.

CARDIOVASCULAR:
Heart size within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. No focal FDG uptake in the myocardium beyond expected physiologic activity. Aorta and great vessels are normal in caliber without aneurysmal change or dissection.

LIVER AND BILIARY SYSTEM:
The liver parenchyma demonstrates heterogeneous attenuation on CT with at least 6 discrete hypodense lesions scattered throughout both lobes, ranging in size from approximately 0.8 cm to 2.4 cm. These lesions show corresponding FDG uptake:
— Segment 6: 2.4 cm, SUVmax 11.8 (dominant lesion)
— Segment 5: 1.7 cm, SUVmax 8.3
— Segment 7: 1.4 cm, SUVmax 7.1
— Segment 4a: 1.1 cm, SUVmax 6.6
— Segment 3: 0.9 cm, SUVmax 5.4
— Segment 8: 0.8 cm, SUVmax 4.9
The lesions are predominantly peripheral and do not demonstrate arterial enhancement or washout pattern typical of hepatocellular carcinoma. Morphology and metabolic activity are consistent with metastatic disease. No biliary ductal dilatation. Gallbladder is unremarkable. Portal vein is patent.

SPLEEN:
Normal in size and attenuation. No splenic lesions. No FDG-avid foci.

ADRENAL GLANDS:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology. No nodules or FDG-avid lesions.

KIDNEYS AND URINARY TRACT:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size with maintained cortical thickness. Bilateral physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems. No solid renal masses or hydronephrosis. Bladder is decompressed and appears normal.

PERITONEUM AND RETROPERITONEUM:
No peritoneal nodules or implants are identified on this study. No ascites. No retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy. No FDG-avid mesenteric or omental disease.

BONES:
No lytic, sclerotic, or FDG-avid osseous lesions identified throughout the axial or appendicular skeleton visualized. No pathologic fractures. Bone marrow FDG activity is within physiologic limits.

SUBCUTANEOUS SOFT TISSUES / SKIN:
Numerous FDG-avid subcutaneous and cutaneous nodules are distributed across the trunk and bilateral upper extremities. These range from approximately 0.5 cm to 3.1 cm. The largest lesion, located in the left lateral chest wall subcutaneous fat, measures 3.1 cm and demonstrates an SUVmax of 14.2. Additional nodules are present overlying the right upper back (2.2 cm, SUVmax 12.0), left shoulder (1.6 cm, SUVmax 9.4), right lateral abdomen (1.1 cm, SUVmax 7.8), and several smaller foci throughout the trunk (0.5-0.9 cm, SUVmax 3.5-6.2). These are consistent with cutaneous and subcutaneous metastatic deposits.

MRI BRAIN:
No leptomeningeal enhancement or pachymeningeal disease. No intra-axial or extra-axial enhancing lesions. No abnormal T2/FLAIR signal to suggest parenchymal involvement. No diffusion restriction. No susceptibility artifacts to suggest hemorrhagic metastasis. Normal ventricular size and sulcal pattern. Visualized cranial nerves unremarkable. No pituitary mass.

IMPRESSION:

1. Widespread metastatic melanoma with involvement of multiple anatomic compartments, consistent with advanced stage IV disease:

   a. Bilateral axillary lymphadenopathy, most prominent on the right (dominant node 4.2 cm, SUVmax 18.4, with CT features suggesting extranodal extension). Left axilla also involved (1.8 cm, SUVmax 9.2).

   b. Bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy: right 2.1 cm (SUVmax 7.6), left 1.4 cm (SUVmax 5.3).

   c. Extensive FDG-avid subcutaneous and cutaneous metastatic nodules distributed over the trunk and bilateral upper extremities, largest 3.1 cm (SUVmax 14.2), with numerous additional nodules.

   d. Multiple hepatic metastases — at least 6 discrete lesions throughout both hepatic lobes, largest 2.4 cm in segment 6 (SUVmax 11.8).

2. No pulmonary parenchymal metastases.

3. No intracranial metastases on concurrent MRI brain with gadolinium.

4. No osseous metastases.

5. No peritoneal, retroperitoneal, or adrenal involvement identified on this examination.

6. Post-biopsy changes at the dorsal trunk primary site; no clearly discrete residual FDG-avid primary tumor mass.

These findings are consistent with the known clinical diagnosis of melanoma with distant spread to multiple nodal stations, the skin/subcutaneous soft tissues, and liver. Clinically correlated as needed for determination of systemic treatment strategy.
